title: Recovery Plan Exercise Attendance and Scenario Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-009-6 R3 Part 3.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-009-6 R3 Part 3.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.46: Recovery Plan Exercise Attendance and Scenario Evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.46' running on index=grc sourcetype=cip009_exercise, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype=cip009_exercise earliest=-400d
| eval ex_epoch=strptime(exercise_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval annual_cutoff=relative_time(now(),"-1y@d")
| stats latest(exercise_type) as last_type latest(exercise_date) as last_date values(participant) as people by bes_system
| eval compliant=if(strptime(last_date,"%Y-%m-%d")>=annual_cutoff,1,0)
| where compliant=0
| table bes_system, last_date, last_type, people
description: We log who ran tabletop or functional recovery exercises and which BES systems were exercised, supporting recovery plan testing evidence.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes cip009_exercise, cip009_exercise`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Push exercise completion events from GRC. (2) Align `bes_system` with CIP-002 list. (3) Alert before annual due. (4) Attach scenario documents out-of-band with links in events. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip009_exercise`, `sourcetype=cip009_exercise``.
visualization: Table (systems needing exercise), Single value (non-compliant count), Timeline

grandmaExplanation: We watch for we log who ran tabletop or functional recovery exercises and which bes systems were exercised, supporting recovery plan testing evidence. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip009_exercise`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Push exercise completion events from GRC. (2) Align `bes_system` with CIP-002 list. (3) Alert before annual due. (4) Attach scenario documents out-of-band with links in events.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip009_exercise earliest=-400d
| eval ex_epoch=strptime(exercise_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval annual_cutoff=relative_time(now(),"-1y@d")
| stats latest(exercise_type) as last_type latest(exercise_date) as last_date values(participant) as people by bes_system
| eval compliant=if(strptime(last_date,"%Y-%m-%d")>=annual_cutoff,1,0)
| where compliant=0
| table bes_system, last_date, last_type, people
```

Understanding this SPL

**Recovery Plan Exercise Attendance and Scenario Evidence (CIP-009-6 R3 Part 3.1)** — We log who ran tabletop or functional recovery exercises and which BES systems were exercised, supporting recovery plan testing evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip009_exercise`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: cip009_exercise. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=cip009_exercise, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ex_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **annual_cutoff** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **bes_system**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **compliant** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where compliant=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: annual_cutoff, bes_system, compliant, ex_epoch, last_date, last_type, people.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (systems needing exercise), Single value (non-compliant count), Timeline
